title: poa pumilio
intro: Poa pumilio, also known as the Alpine Fescue, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of the South Island in New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Poa pumilio include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
